exemplar.title: How does the electrical power supplied to a nickel-chromium heating element affect its output temperature as measured by a thermal camera at a fixed distance?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the electrical power supplied to a nickel-chromium heating element affect its output temperature as measured by a thermal camera at a fixed distance?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs communicate recording and processing both clearly and precisely, with units and uncertainties.
Uncertainty propagation for temperature and instrument errors is correctly applied.
Inclusion of spectrometer data to explore Wienâs law enriches the analysis.
weaknesses: Uncertainty estimates for regression slope and intercept are omitted.
Minor rounding inconsistencies in Tâ´ values suggest processing errors.
Rationale for deletion of low-temperature points and quantification of systematic effects are lacking.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data is in the top band (5), data processing shows only minor omissions (4), and uncertainty consideration falls in the 3â4 band (3). Balancing clarity and precision with gaps in uncertainty quantification yields a score of 4.

strengths: Concise, directly testable hypothesis linked to the StefanâBoltzmann relationship.
Detailed apparatus photographs, clear schematic and numbered procedure support reproducibility.
Thorough control-variable descriptions for length, thickness and distance.
weaknesses: Context remains broad without a clearly delimited real-world application.
Omission of critical details (e.g., thermal-camera emissivity setting and orientation) hinders full reproducibility.
No justification given for convergence criterion (Â±1 K over 10 s).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place context and methodology in the 3â4 band (clear context but not highly specific; reproducible with few ambiguities) and methodological considerations at the lower 3â4 band. The work goes beyond a basic outline but omits key specifics. A holistic score of 4 reflects solid design with room to deepen context and methodological explanation.

strengths: Conclusion succinctly answers the research question and cites the high RÂ² as supporting evidence.
Clear restatement of empirical verification of P â Tâ´, aligning with the analysis.
weaknesses: Conclusion does not integrate uncertainty in the regression gradient or discuss the impact of removed data points quantitatively.
Literature comparisons cite relevant examples but lack critical numerical contrast with experiment.
Misses discussion of regression uncertainties or intercept significance when drawing conclusions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion directly answers the research question and is consistent with analysis (band 3â4) but lacks full justificationâparticularly the quantitative integration of uncertainties and critical literature comparisonâplacing it at the lower half of the 3â4 band. A score of 3 appropriately reflects these strengths and gaps.

strengths: Identifies specific methodological weaknesses (ambient temperature, emissivity variation, convection effects).
Proposes realistic improvements (vacuum testing, extended temperature range, varied wire dimensions).
Insightful discussion linking the y-intercept to ambient-condition errors.
weaknesses: Does not explain the relative impact of each identified limitation on the results.
No prioritization or feasibility assessment for proposed improvements.
Lacks formal criteria (e.g., statistical test) for outlier removal.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands (weaknesses description and suggested improvements) fall in the 3â4 band: specific and relevant but without explaining relative impacts or providing detailed feasibility/prioritization. A score of 3 reflects a considered evaluation that could be deepened by quantification and explanation of proposal effectiveness.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student situates the question within the general context of household heaters and nichrome filaments, but the discussion remains fairly broad (cost of heating, Joule heating) and does not link the specific wire, gauge or thermal-camera set-up to a clearly delimited real-world situation.  Hence the context is present but not yet highly specific or targeted, fitting the 3-4 band rather than 5-6.

Uncertainties are propagated for temperature and Tâ´, and percentage methods are explained, but the power values, slope and RÂ² lack associated uncertainties.  Systematic effects are acknowledged qualitatively, yet not quantified.  This represents âsome consideration â¦ with significant omissions,â aligning with the 3-4 band (score 3).

The student identifies relevant controls (length, gauge, distance) and briefly states why they matter, but the discussion of sufficiency is limited; for example, ambient temperature and convection are recognised only later in the report and not managed during data collection.  Thus methodological considerations are described rather than fully explained, meriting the upper half of the 3-4 band but not 5-6.

Ambient temperature, emissivity deviation and convection are specifically identified, but the relative magnitude of each is only hinted at.  The impact on gradient and y-intercept is discussed qualitatively, representing a description rather than an explanation, hence band 3-4 (score 3).

Apparatus photographs, a schematic, a numbered procedure and explicit variable tables allow another investigator to reproduce the work with only minor ambiguity (e.g. exact thermal-camera settings, ambient-temperature monitoring).  Detail goes beyond a cursory outline but still omits a few particulars (wire mounting tension, camera emissivity setting), matching the descriptor "reproduced with few ambiguities" (3-4 band).

Averages, transformation to Tâ´, linear regression and removal of low-temperature points are mathematically sound.  Minor issues (rounding errors, lack of propagated uncertainty in gradient, questionable deletion of data) indicate some inaccuracies but not major ones, fitting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Proposed improvements (conducting experiment in a vacuum/low temperature, extending temperature range, varying wire dimensions) are realistic and clearly linked to the stated weaknesses, but their feasibility, expected degree of improvement, or prioritisation are not explained.  Therefore the strand fits the 3-4 band with a score of 3.

Raw tables include units, uncertainties and multiple trials; processed values (averages, Tâ´) and graphical representations are clearly laid out.  Although layout is occasionally cramped, both clarity and precision are generally achieved, justifying placement in the 5-6 band (score 5 rather than 6 owing to minor formatting inconsistencies).

Links to Stefan-Boltzmann theory and comparisons with bulb filaments and laser-heated bone provide relevant context, yet depth is limited; numerical values from literature are not critically contrasted with experimental outcomes.  This meets âsome relevant comparisonâ (score 3).

The conclusion directly answers the research question and cites the high RÂ² value as evidence.  It remains consistent with the processed data but does not integrate uncertainty in the gradient or discuss the removed pointsâ influence quantitatively, so it is âsupportedâ rather than fully âjustifiedâ, aligning with 3-4 (score 4).

annotations.comment: Equating electrical power to radiated luminosity in Eq. (2) assumes no losses by conduction or convection; discuss or quantify this omission.
The hypothesis is concise and directly testable against the StefanâBoltzmann relationship.
The methodology outlines controls for length and gauge but omits thermal camera settings (emissivity, focus), hindering full reproducibility.
The apparatus photo and schematic are clear, aiding reproducibility, but camera orientation relative to wire should be labelled.
Use of precise voltmeter and ammeter readings to reduce systematic error is an excellent methodological consideration.
The background effectively explains Joule heating at the atomic scale, demonstrating solid theoretical grounding.
The research question is clearly stated, but lacks linkage to a specific real-world scenario or application, limiting contextual depth.
Equation (1) is introduced without first defining all symbols in the preceding text; move variable definitions ahead of the formula.
Outlier removal of the first two points lacks formal criteria; apply a statistical test (e.g., Grubbs') before deletion.
Discussion of the y-intercept as ambient condition error is insightful, showing strong link between data and theory.
Independent-variable increments are chosen for range but rationale should link to thermal camera resolution and sensitivity.
Ensuring equilibrium within Â±1 K over 10 s is prudent, but justify why Â±1 K is an acceptable convergence criterion.
Control-variable descriptions for length, thickness, and distance are thorough and well justified.
Measurement distance is noted qualitatively; add a calibration or manufacturer data to quantify its impact.
Experimental steps are well-structured and numbered, providing clear instructions for reproducing procedures.
Materials list includes instrument uncertainties, supporting transparent uncertainty propagation.
The spectrometer data extends the study to Wien's law, enriching the analysis with complementary evidence.
Safety considerations are thorough but omit the need to account for reflective IR from surroundings affecting camera readings.
Raw data table clearly presents multiple trials, averages, and uncertainties, illustrating precise data communication.
Some rounding inconsistencies in Tâ´ values suggest minor processing errors; verify significant figures match propagated uncertainties.
The FLIR limit at 120 Â°C is acknowledged; consider extending range or using alternative sensor for higher powers.
Uncertainty propagation via range vs instrument error is correctly applied, demonstrating good awareness of random vs systematic sources.
The report omits uncertainty estimates for regression slope and intercept; include these to fully address uncertainty consideration.
Graph 1 interpretation connects RÂ² to correlation strength, but would benefit from explicitly stating the implications for P â Tâ´.
Post-removal regression analysis yields unrealistic Îµ; revisit assumed geometry and emissivity constancy, and quantify wire surface properties.
The conclusion succinctly restates the empirical verification of P â Tâ´, demonstrating clear alignment with the research question.
Reference to the r-crit value adds statistical rigor, but integrate this earlier when discussing regression fit.
Literature comparisons cite relevant studies but do not critically compare numerical gradients or intercepts to your values.
Strengths section effectively highlights preliminary trial selection and equipment controls, demonstrating thoughtful planning.
Limitations identify room-temperature environment impact but do not quantify how each systematic factor shifts the slope or intercept.
Proposed extensions to vacuum testing and varied wire dimensions are realistic and directly address identified limitations.
Works Cited is comprehensive, but omission of a thermal camera manual or emissivity reference should be addressed.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
